decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak temperature in the chip region.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation.
